texto: Develop in your heart the feeling of love for your people and let it be the source of kindliness and blessing to them. Do not behave with them like a barbarian, and do not appropriate to yourself that which belongs to them. Remember that the citizens of the state are of two categories. They are either your brethren in religion or your brethren in kind. They are subject to infirmities and liable to commit mistakes.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Hazrat Ali Ibn Abu-Talib A.S: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Develop in your heart the feeling of love for your people and let it be the source of kindliness …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, ali-letter-to-malikm, humanism pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de humanism e love.

Do not behave with them like a barbarian, and do not appropriate to yourself that which belongs to them. Remember that the citizens of the state are of two categories. texto: Beware! Abstain from shedding blood without a valid cause. There is nothing more harmful than this which brings about one’s ruin. The blood that is willfully shed shortens the life of a state. On the Day of Judgement it is this crime for which one will have to answer first. So, beware! Do not wish to build the strength of your state on blood for, it is this blood which ultimately weakens the state and passes it into other hands. Before me and my God no excuse for willful killing can be entertained.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Hazrat Ali Ibn Abu-Talib A.S sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “Beware! Abstain from shedding blood without a valid cause. There is nothing more harmful than thi…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, ali-letter-to-malik, blood, útil em momentos de blood e justice.
